failure_surface: Agent wrote static datetime values to cells instead of creating Excel formulas. The task explicitly requested 'a formula in column U' but the agent used Python to write fixed values.
root_cause: The agent misunderstood the task requirement. The user explicitly asked 'How can I create a formula' but the agent implemented static value insertion via openpyxl.cell.value assignment rather than writing formula strings to cells.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When a task request explicitly mentions creating/formula/query, use formula string syntax (e.g., '=INDEX(...)') instead of writing evaluated values. Check for keywords like 'formula', 'calculate', 'query', or 'dynamic' in instructions before deciding between static vs. formula approach.
evidence: User instruction states: 'How can I create a formula in column U of my stock control spreadsheet'
User instruction states: 'i need a formula in column U which bring the header value in it if that column has got a number value'
Agent code assigned u_cell.value = month_to_date.get(month_name, '') - direct value assignment, not a formula
No formulas were written to cells; verification shows type: datetime for all U cells
Task requires Excel 2010 compatibility - MAXIFS not available, needs alternative formula like INDEX/MATCH/COLLECT

memory_items.title: Formula vs Value Intent Detection
memory_items.lesson: Always distinguish between requests to create formulas versus insert static values. Keywords like 'create a formula', 'show', 'display based on condition', or 'calculate' indicate formula requirements.
memory_items.generalization: Instruct agents to parse task language for formula intent indicators before choosing write strategy. Write formulas as strings (e.g., '=FORMULA()') rather than pre-computed values when user explicitly requests formula creation.

memory_items.title: Legacy Excel Compatibility Awareness
memory_items.lesson: When tasks mention version limitations (e.g., 'Excel 2010'), verify function availability. Modern functions like MAXIFS may not exist in older versions.
memory_items.generalization: Agents should identify version constraints in instructions and either document them or construct compatible alternatives (e.g., INDEX+MATCH, IF statements) instead of relying on newer library features.

memory_items.title: Dynamic vs Static Output Validation
memory_items.lesson: After completing formula-based tasks, verify that target cells contain formula strings rather than static values. Use ws.cell().value to check for '=' prefix.
memory_items.generalization: Add validation step: after writing to formula-requested ranges, confirm cells start with '=' to ensure formulas were written, not computed values.

edits.content: - **Formula Intent Detection**: Parse instructions for keywords indicating formula creation ('create a formula', 'calculate', 'dynamically show'). When detected, write formulas as raw strings starting with '=' rather than evaluating and storing static values. Verify Excel version compatibility for proposed functions (e.g., MAXIFS only available in Excel 2016+).
